headline: Software stocks tumble: High memory prices cannibalize enterprise tech capex
summary: Tech stocks faced downward pressure Tuesday as disappointing preliminary results from IBM (NYSE:IBM) rippled across the sector, pulling shares of major software companies lower. Following IBM’s update, investors reacted sharply to the cooling sentiment in the broader software space: Accenture (NYSE:ACN): Fell 8% ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW): Dropped 6% Workday (NASDAQ:WDAY): Declined 5% Salesforce (NYSE:CRM): Slipped 5% The decline is rooted in a fundamental shift in enterprise capital expenditure (cap

headline: Accenture (ACN) Curbs Routine AI Use As Token Costs Force Tighter Focus
summary: Accenture has restricted employee use of generative AI for routine tasks to manage rising token costs. The company is shifting toward more targeted AI deployment, focusing on higher value internal and client use cases. This move reflects a broader reassessment across enterprises of how and where AI is applied in everyday workflows. For investors tracking NYSE:ACN, this AI usage reset comes as the stock trades around $138.52, with the share price down 18.7% over the past month and down 46.7%...

headline: The Detail in IBM’s Earnings Pre-Announcement That Has Stock Headed for Worst Day in Nearly 40 Years
summary: IBM  stock headed for its largest same-day plunge in nearly 40 years on Tuesday after the company’s preliminary second-quarter adjusted earnings and revenue missed analysts’ targets.  In a letter to shareholders, CEO Arvind Krishna explained that the shortfall was largely driven by the company’s infrastructure business.  “What played out was worse than our expectations,” Krishna wrote.

content.title: The Detail in IBM’s Earnings Pre-Announcement That Has Stock Headed for Worst Day in Nearly 40 Years
content.summary: IBM  stock headed for its largest same-day plunge in nearly 40 years on Tuesday after the company’s preliminary second-quarter adjusted earnings and revenue missed analysts’ targets.  In a letter to shareholders, CEO Arvind Krishna explained that the shortfall was largely driven by the company’s infrastructure business.  “What played out was worse than our expectations,” Krishna wrote.

headline: HSBC downgrades IBM to Reduce, says "synthetic IBM" offers better value
summary: Investing.com -- HSBC downgraded IBM to Reduce from Hold and cut its price target to $191 from $231 in a note on Tuesday, arguing that a basket of IBM's peers offers superior earnings potential for the same investment.

content.title: HSBC downgrades IBM to Reduce, says "synthetic IBM" offers better value
content.summary: Investing.com -- HSBC downgraded IBM to Reduce from Hold and cut its price target to $191 from $231 in a note on Tuesday, arguing that a basket of IBM's peers offers superior earnings potential for the same investment.

headline: NATO Cloud Deal And Mid‑Market AI Push Could Be A Game Changer For Accenture (ACN)
summary: In early July 2026, NATO’s Communications and Information Agency awarded Accenture a roughly €200 million, seven‑year Protected Business Network contract, delivered with Leonardo to modernize NATO’s secure multi‑cloud digital infrastructure for about 29,000 users. Accenture also announced new agentic AI solutions with Google Cloud for mid‑market clients, while sector‑wide worries about shifting IT and AI spending patterns have raised fresh questions about how consulting firms will evolve...

headline: The Detail in IBM’s Earnings Pre-Announcement That Has The Stock Headed for Its Worst Day Ever
summary: IBM  stock was pacing toward its worst day ever on Tuesday after the company’s preliminary second-quarter earnings missed analysts’ targets.  In a letter to shareholders, CEO Arvind Krishna explained that the shortfall was largely driven by the company’s infrastructure business.  “What played out was worse than our expectations,” Krishna wrote.
